title: Need a way to specify gpg key homedir
body:
```
[root@localhost ~]# skopeo standalone-sign /tmp/skopeo/dir/manifest.json docker-daemon:docker.io/busybox:latest 5B4476EC3C2C1394E33DAA478A2DC83509B491E4 -o busybox.signature
FATA[0000] Error creating signature: key "5B4476EC3C2C1394E33DAA478A2DC83509B491E4" not found 
[root@localhost ~]# gpg --fingerprint --homedir /tmp/skopeo/gpg --with-colons --no-permission-warning | grep fpr | awk -F ':'  '{print $10}'
5B4476EC3C2C1394E33DAA478A2DC83509B491E4
[root@localhost ~]# gpg --fingerprint --homedir /tmp/skopeo/gpg --with-colons --no-permission-warning 
tru::1:1512385848:0:3:1:5
pub:u:2048:1:8A2DC83509B491E4:2017-12-04:::u:Guohua Ouyang <gouyang@redhat.com>::escaESCA:
fpr:::::::::5B4476EC3C2C1394E33DAA478A2DC83509B491E4:
sub:u:2048:1:AC6F40EF49E9C088:2017-12-04::::::esa:
```

comments.body:
Does setting $HOME environment help?

HOME=/tmp/skopeo skopeo standalone-sign /tmp/skopeo/dir/manifest.json docker-daemon:docker.io/busybox:latest 5B4476EC3C2C1394E33DAA478A2DC83509B491E4 -o busybox.signature

`HOME` and `GNUPGHOME` not work.

```
[root@fedora skopeo]# GNUPGHOME=/tmp/skopeo/gpg ./skopeo standalone-sign /tmp/skopeo/dir/manifest.json docker-daemon:docker.io/busybox:latest 5B4476EC3C2C1394E33DAA478A2DC83509B491E4 -o busybox.signature
FATA[0000] Error creating signature: key "5B4476EC3C2C1394E33DAA478A2DC83509B491E4" not found 
[root@fedora skopeo]# HOME=/tmp/skopeo/gpg ./skopeo standalone-sign /tmp/skopeo/dir/manifest.json docker-daemon:docker.io/busybox:latest 5B4476EC3C2C1394E33DAA478A2DC83509B491E4 -o busybox.signature
FATA[0000] Error creating signature: key "5B4476EC3C2C1394E33DAA478A2DC83509B491E4" not found 
```

This really should work, we have [tests for that](https://github.com/projectatomic/skopeo/blob/master/integration/signing_test.go) .

And it does for me:
```
$ GNUPGHOME=$(pwd)/t gpg --gen-key
…
$ GNUPGHOME=$(pwd)/t gpg --fingerprint --with-colons
gpg: VAROVÁNÍ: přístupová práva pro domovský adresáře nejsou bezpečná `/home/mitr/Go/src/github.com/projectatomic/skopeo/t'
tru::1:1512435337:0:3:1:5
pub:u:2048:1:4DD5BBA4318238F0:2017-12-05:::u:test test::scESC:
fpr:::::::::A8905B10DB2E465E6AC6291C4DD5BBA4318238F0:
sub:u:2048:1:77AA20E5F30FD250:2017-12-05::::::e:
$ GNUPGHOME=$(pwd)/t ./skopeo standalone-sign /dev/null busybox A8905B10DB2E465E6AC6291C4DD5BBA4318238F0 -o foo
$ gpg --list-packets < foo
…
:signature packet: algo 1, keyid 4DD5BBA4318238F0
…
```
Adding `GPGME_DEBUG=7` (or a higher level) in the environment might help diagnose the issue: it shows the relevant home directory, and the output of `gpg` commands.
yes, it works. I didn't notice the key is changed and I still use the old key to test it.
